Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query "Zara Okoye" or "Mosaic damage" or "damage claim" or "Tanaka" (or similar) to find correspondence about the Mosaic prototype damage and equipment issues. Expected findings: An email from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com (CC emeka.diallo@moveops.com) with subject "Following up: Zara Okoye damage incident report + Keiko Tanaka lens issue" sent Friday April 24. Tamara states the formal incident report is overdue (original deadline April 17), Meridian Risk Partners will open an independent investigation if the report is not received by end of business Monday, April 27, and she wants the report to include packing verification, pickup-to-delivery timeline, who signed the delivery receipt, and MoveOps root cause assessment. The same email also contains a second issue: Keiko Tanaka received her Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter in Seattle and the lens cap assembly has a hairline crack consistent with impact during transit, repair estimate $2,200 from the manufacturer. Tamara asks who was responsible for the packing inspection on the Tanaka shipment and whether MoveOps or the carrier bears responsibility.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with query "incident report" or "prototype" or "chain of custody" (or similar) to find the incident report Emeka previously sent to Tamara. Expected findings: A sent email from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com (CC rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com) containing a detailed incident report on the Zara Okoye prototype damage. Discovery includes: $90K robotic arm (Unit #1) damaged during Savannah-to-Atlanta move on April 14-15, cracked servo motor housing and joint calibration misalignment, Heartland Movers crew lead Marcus Delaney, Blessing Okafor countersigned packing checklist remotely while physically in Philadelphia coordinating Tyler Okonkwo's move, delivery receipt signed by a Mosaic intern (not Zara), and $40K insurance coverage gap (vendor liability covers $50K per item but damage is $90K).
OE 3: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Tanaka" or "DOT" or "placarding" or "certificate" (or similar), or browse #operations using conversations_history with channel_id "C006", to find the DOT backdating concern. Expected findings: A message from moveops_javier_morales in #operations (C006) posted Friday April 24 stating that Greg Pallone at Swift sent the signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment, but the certificate is dated April 22 while the actual shipment moved April 15, creating a 7-day gap. Javier questions whether DOT will accept a retroactively signed certificate for the PHMSA case file and suggests it could be worse than admitting the gap. He asks Blessing for driver handoff notes from the Tanaka shipment day.
OE 4: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Marcus Webb" or "Canopy" or "Patricia" or "timeline" (or similar), or browse #operations using conversations_history with channel_id "C006", to find the Canopy timeline pressure message. Expected findings: A message from moveops_chloe_vance in #operations (C006) posted Saturday April 25 stating Patricia Langford at Canopy Health says Marcus Webb must be physically in Chicago at the Canopy lab by Monday May 4, cryo shipment must arrive by Friday May 1 (ideally Thursday April 30), Blessing must finalize the split-vendor plan by Monday morning, and the lab dock at Canopy Chicago is at 200 W Adams St, Dock B, Lower Level, with receiving hours Monday-Friday 7 AM to 3 PM, requiring 48-hour advance scheduling notice.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with query "cold-chain" or "Swift" or "freight quote" or "cryogenic" (or similar) to find the specialized freight quote. Expected findings: An email from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com) with subject "RE: Cold-chain freight quote for Marcus Webb (Canopy Health) Detroit to Chicago" sent Friday April 24. The quote is $4,850 total, broken down as: base freight rate $3,200, cryogenic monitoring equipment rental $450, DOT hazmat placarding $350, biological specimen chain-of-custody handling surcharge $550, loading/unloading with cryogenic PPE crew $300. Insurance caps at $25,000 per item. Requires signed chain-of-custody waiver from receiving lab manager. Earliest cold-chain truck for Detroit pickup is Wednesday April 29. Lab dock delivery requires 48-hour advance notice.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with query "Heartland" or "household" or "Marcus Webb" or "pickup address" (or similar) to find the household move confirmation and address mismatch. Expected findings: An email from lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com with subject "RE: Marcus Webb household move, Detroit to Chicago" sent Friday April 24. Lisa confirms standard residential rate of $1,600 flat for ~300 cubic feet. She flags an address mismatch: the pickup address on file is 4215 Woodward Ave, Apt 6C, Detroit, MI 48201 (from MoveOps intake form), but the Canopy HR packet lists Apt 4B at the same building. She also needs the Chicago delivery address, which currently shows "Chicago apartment, address TBD." She needs both addresses confirmed by Monday to schedule a crew.
OE 7: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query "Canopy" or "Marchetti" or "Webb" (or similar) to find tracking tickets related to these relocations. Expected findings: issue_canopy_marchetti_apt (title: "Apartment Search -- Lily Marchetti -- Chicago (Canopy Health)"), assigned to moveops_blessing_okafor, on project proj_canopy. The agent should then retrieve comments on this issue using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_canopy_marchetti_apt" to discover Chloe Vance's injected comment detailing the Swift cold-chain quote of $4,850 vs the $1,200 budgeted on the Canopy invoice (INV-2026-0401), and listing 5 action items for Blessing: (1) confirm with Catalina the $3,650 cost overrun discussion with Patricia, (2) verify Marcus Webb's Detroit pickup address (Apt 6C vs Apt 4B), (3) get Chicago apartment delivery address for Heartland, (4) notify Canopy lab dock by Tuesday April 28 for Thursday April 30 delivery, (5) update Airtable record with confirmed addresses and revised Swift quote.
OE 8: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query "DOT" or "Tanaka" or "placarding" (or similar) to find the DOT compliance tickets. Expected findings: issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (title: "DOT Docs -- Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser"), assigned to moveops_chloe_vance, priority 1, state in_progress, on project project_dot_compliance_audit. Also issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (title: "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations -- URGENT"), priority 1, state todo. Discovery: the placarding certificate gap was originally flagged by Blessing, Chloe contacted Swift to obtain a retroactive copy, and Greg confirmed they would provide it. The PHMSA submission deadline is June 5.
OE 9: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id "base_moveops_ops" (or "appMoveOpsOps001"), table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Marcus Webb" (or similar) to find Marcus Webb's relocation records. Expected findings: recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago with Company "Canopy Health", Status "In Progress", Origin "Detroit, MI", Destination "Chicago, IL", Assigned Coordinator "Blessing Okafor", Move Start Date "2026-04-10", Move End Date "2026-04-12" (stale dates), Special Requirements describing the split-vendor arrangement (Heartland for household, Swift for cold-chain lab equipment). Also a second Marcus Webb record (recMarcusWebbBrightloop or rec_reloc_webb) for a BrightLoop Analytics relocation, which is a different person/move.
OE 10: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id "base_moveops_ops" (or "appMoveOpsOps001"), table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Zara Okoye" (or similar). Expected findings: recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta with Company "Mosaic Robotics", Status "Completed", Assigned Coordinator "Blessing Okafor", Special Requirements describing 2x robotic arm prototypes ($180K total insured value), custom anti-vibration foam crating, climate-controlled transport.
OE 11: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id "base_moveops_ops" (or "appMoveOpsOps001"), table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Keiko Tanaka" (or similar). Expected findings: recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle with Company "Mosaic Robotics", Status "In Progress", Assigned Coordinator "Javier Morales", Special Requirements describing the Epilog Fusion Pro 48 industrial laser cutter (400 lbs, ~$35K value) with Class 3B CO2 laser tube, DOT hazmat placarding required, Swift Relocations as carrier.
OE 12: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "Canopy" or "Webb" (or similar) to find the Canopy Marcus Webb deal and verify budget figures. Expected findings: deal_canopy_webb_apr2026 with amount $3,200, dealstage "inprogress", description showing Blessing Okafor as coordinator, split-vendor arrangement (Heartland household + Swift cold-chain), and the budgeted cold-chain equipment freight surcharge of $1,200 from the Canopy invoice.
OE 13: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "Mosaic" or "Okoye" (or similar) to find Mosaic deals. Expected findings: deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026 (original deal, $2,700) and deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk (RISK: DAMAGE CLAIM flag), showing $90K prototype damage, $40K insurance coverage gap (vendor liability covers $50K per item), Heartland Movers as carrier, Blessing Okafor as coordinator. Also deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 ($2,770, Javier Morales as coordinator, Swift Relocations, DOT hazmat requirements).
OE 14: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria for Canopy Health (or similar) to verify the budget discrepancy. Expected findings: Invoice 1009 (DocNumber INV-2026-0401) for Canopy Health Q2 relocations totaling $9,210, with line items including a cold-chain equipment freight surcharge of $1,200 for Marcus Webb, confirming the $3,650 gap between the $1,200 budgeted and the $4,850 actual Swift quote.
OE 15: Look up Contacts to identify the Canopy account manager. Use contacts_search_contacts with query "Catalina" (or similar), or crm_search_contacts with full_name "Catalina Dubois" (or similar). Expected findings: Catalina Dubois, Account Manager, catalina.dubois@moveops.com. She is the account manager for Canopy Health and the point of contact who needs to discuss the $3,650 cost overrun with Patricia Langford.
OE 16: Look up Contacts to identify the Mosaic account manager. Use contacts_search_contacts with query "Emeka" (or similar), or crm_search_contacts with full_name "Emeka Diallo" (or similar). Expected findings: Emeka Diallo, Account Manager, emeka.diallo@moveops.com. He manages the Mosaic Robotics relationship and was CC'd on Tamara Byrd's email about the damage claim and Tanaka lens issue.
OE 17: Update the Marcus Webb Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records with base_id "base_moveops_ops" (or "appMoveOpsOps001"), table_id "tblRelocations01", records containing id "recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago" with updated fields. Updates should include: revised Swift cold-chain quote ($4,850), address discrepancy notes (Apt 6C vs Apt 4B pending verification), Chicago lab dock address (200 W Adams St, Dock B), revised timeline (pickup April 29, delivery April 30, Marcus on-site May 4), and note that Catalina must confirm budget overrun with Patricia before Swift booking is locked.
OE 18: Update the Zara Okoye Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records with base_id "base_moveops_ops" (or "appMoveOpsOps001"), table_id "tblRelocations01", records containing id "recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta" with updated Special Requirements noting the damage claim deadline (Meridian Risk Partners, end of business Monday April 27), the $40K insurance gap, and current status of the incident report.
OE 19: Update the Keiko Tanaka Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records with base_id "base_moveops_ops" (or "appMoveOpsOps001"), table_id "tblRelocations01", records containing id "recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle" with updated Special Requirements noting the lens cap crack ($2,200 repair estimate), transit damage finding, and the DOT placarding certificate backdating concern (dated April 22, shipment April 15).
OE 20: Post a summary message in #operations using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C006", user_id "moveops_blessing_okafor" (or similar), payload containing a consolidated status summary covering: (1) Mosaic Zara Okoye damage claim deadline (Monday April 27, Meridian Risk), incident report status, $40K insurance gap; (2) Mosaic Keiko Tanaka lens cap crack ($2,200), backdated DOT placarding cert concern; (3) Canopy Marcus Webb split-vendor status: Swift cold-chain quote $4,850 vs $1,200 budgeted, Detroit pickup address mismatch (Apt 6C vs 4B), timeline constraints (May 4 lab deadline, April 29 pickup, April 28 dock notification), Catalina needs to discuss budget overrun with Patricia before Swift booking can proceed.
OE 21: Create a Linear tracking issue for the Marcus Webb split-vendor coordination using linear_create_issue with team "team_operations" (or "team_relocations"), title referencing Marcus Webb Canopy cold-chain coordination blockers (or similar), description covering the key blockers: $4,850 vs $1,200 budget gap pending Catalina/Patricia approval, pickup address discrepancy (Apt 6C vs Apt 4B), Chicago delivery address TBD, dock notification deadline (April 28), and Swift pickup window (April 29). Assign to moveops_blessing_okafor (or similar).
OE 22: Create a Linear tracking issue for the Zara Okoye damage claim deadline using linear_create_issue with team "team_operations" (or "team_relocations"), title referencing Okoye damage claim or Meridian Risk deadline (or similar), description covering the Monday April 27 deadline, $40K insurance gap, and need for formal incident report to be provided to Tamara Byrd. Assign to moveops_blessing_okafor or moveops_emeka_diallo (or similar).
OE 23: Create a Linear tracking issue for the Keiko Tanaka lens cap damage using linear_create_issue with team "team_operations" (or "team_relocations"), title referencing Tanaka lens cap or equipment damage (or similar), description covering the $2,200 repair estimate, transit damage determination, and question of MoveOps vs carrier responsibility for packing inspection. Assign to moveops_blessing_okafor or moveops_javier_morales (or similar).
OE 24: Update the existing DOT compliance Linear issue for Keiko Tanaka using linear_update_issue with id "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic" or linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic", adding information about Javier's concern that the retroactive placarding certificate is dated April 22 while the shipment moved April 15, and noting this could undermine the PHMSA submission if DOT treats a backdated cert as equivalent to no cert.
OE 25: Send an email (via send_email or reply_to_email) to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com from blessing.okafor@moveops.com regarding the cold-chain freight pickup. Content should confirm interest in the April 29 pickup slot for the Marcus Webb Detroit-to-Chicago cold-chain shipment, reference the $4,850 quote (pending internal budget approval), provide the lab dock delivery address (200 W Adams St, Dock B, Chicago), note the 48-hour advance scheduling requirement, and request confirmation that the April 29 pickup and April 30 delivery timeline works.
OE 26: Send an email (via send_email or reply_to_email) to lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com from blessing.okafor@moveops.com regarding the household move address confirmation. Content should acknowledge the address mismatch (Apt 6C vs Apt 4B), indicate which address is being verified, and note that the Chicago delivery address is still being confirmed. This fulfills the prompt ask to send vendor confirmations that are still hanging.
OE 27: Send a heads-up email or Slack message to Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com) about the Canopy situation. Via send_email from blessing.okafor@moveops.com to catalina.dubois@moveops.com, or via conversations_add_message in an appropriate channel or DM. Content should summarize: the Swift cold-chain quote came in at $4,850 vs the $1,200 budgeted, creating a $3,650 gap that needs to be discussed with Patricia Langford at Canopy before the booking can be locked; the pickup address discrepancy; and the tight timeline (Marcus must be at the lab by May 4, cryo delivery by May 1, pickup April 29).
OE 28: Send a heads-up email or Slack message to Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com) about the Mosaic situation. Via send_email from blessing.okafor@moveops.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com, or via conversations_add_message in an appropriate channel or DM. Content should summarize: the Zara Okoye damage claim deadline is Monday April 27 (Meridian Risk will open an independent investigation if the report is not received), the Keiko Tanaka lens cap crack ($2,200 repair), and the DOT placarding certificate backdating concern that Javier raised.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query "Zara Okoye" or "Mosaic damage" or "damage claim" or "Tanaka" (or similar) to find correspondence about the Mosaic prototype damage and equipment issues. Expected findings: An email from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com (CC emeka.diallo@moveops.com) with subject "Following up: Zara Okoye damage incident report + Keiko Tanaka lens issue" sent Friday April 24. Tamara states the formal incident report is overdue (original deadline April 17), Meridian Risk Partners will open an independent investigation if the report is not received by end of business Monday, April 27, and she wants the report to include packing verification, pickup-to-delivery timeline, who signed the delivery receipt, and MoveOps root cause assessment. The same email also contains a second issue: Keiko Tanaka received her Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter in Seattle and the lens cap assembly has a hairline crack consistent with impact during transit, repair estimate $2,200 from the manufacturer. Tamara asks who was responsible for the packing inspection on the Tanaka shipment and whether MoveOps or the carrier bears responsibility.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with query "incident report" or "prototype" or "chain of custody" (or similar) to find the incident report Emeka previously sent to Tamara. Expected findings: A sent email from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com (CC rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com) containing a detailed incident report on the Zara Okoye prototype damage. Discovery includes: $90K robotic arm (Unit #1) damaged during Savannah-to-Atlanta move on April 14-15, cracked servo motor housing and joint calibration misalignment, Heartland Movers crew lead Marcus Delaney, Blessing Okafor countersigned packing checklist remotely while physically in Philadelphia coordinating Tyler Okonkwo's move, delivery receipt signed by a Mosaic intern (not Zara), and $40K insurance coverage gap (vendor liability covers $50K per item but damage is $90K).
OE 3: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Tanaka" or "DOT" or "placarding" or "certificate" (or similar), or browse #operations using conversations_history with channel_id "C006", to find the DOT backdating concern. Expected findings: A message from moveops_javier_morales in #operations (C006) posted Friday April 24 stating that Greg Pallone at Swift sent the signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment, but the certificate is dated April 22 while the actual shipment moved April 15, creating a 7-day gap. Javier questions whether DOT will accept a retroactively signed certificate for the PHMSA case file and suggests it could be worse than admitting the gap. He asks Blessing for driver handoff notes from the Tanaka shipment day.
OE 4: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Marcus Webb" or "Canopy" or "Patricia" or "timeline" (or similar), or browse #operations using conversations_history with channel_id "C006", to find the Canopy timeline pressure message. Expected findings: A message from moveops_chloe_vance in #operations (C006) posted Saturday April 25 stating Patricia Langford at Canopy Health says Marcus Webb must be physically in Chicago at the Canopy lab by Monday May 4, cryo shipment must arrive by Friday May 1 (ideally Thursday April 30), Blessing must finalize the split-vendor plan by Monday morning, and the lab dock at Canopy Chicago is at 200 W Adams St, Dock B, Lower Level, with receiving hours Monday-Friday 7 AM to 3 PM, requiring 48-hour advance scheduling notice.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with query "cold-chain" or "Swift" or "freight quote" or "cryogenic" (or similar) to find the specialized freight quote. Expected findings: An email from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com) with subject "RE: Cold-chain freight quote for Marcus Webb (Canopy Health) Detroit to Chicago" sent Friday April 24. The quote is $4,850 total, broken down as: base freight rate $3,200, cryogenic monitoring equipment rental $450, DOT hazmat placarding $350, biological specimen chain-of-custody handling surcharge $550, loading/unloading with cryogenic PPE crew $300. Insurance caps at $25,000 per item. Requires signed chain-of-custody waiver from receiving lab manager. Earliest cold-chain truck for Detroit pickup is Wednesday April 29. Lab dock delivery requires 48-hour advance notice.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with query "Heartland" or "household" or "Marcus Webb" or "pickup address" (or similar) to find the household move confirmation and address mismatch. Expected findings: An email from lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com with subject "RE: Marcus Webb household move, Detroit to Chicago" sent Friday April 24. Lisa confirms standard residential rate of $1,600 flat for ~300 cubic feet. She flags an address mismatch: the pickup address on file is 4215 Woodward Ave, Apt 6C, Detroit, MI 48201 (from MoveOps intake form), but the Canopy HR packet lists Apt 4B at the same building. She also needs the Chicago delivery address, which currently shows "Chicago apartment, address TBD." She needs both addresses confirmed by Monday to schedule a crew.
OE 7: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query "Canopy" or "Marchetti" or "Webb" (or similar) to find tracking tickets related to these relocations. Expected findings: issue_canopy_marchetti_apt (title: "Apartment Search -- Lily Marchetti -- Chicago (Canopy Health)"), assigned to moveops_blessing_okafor, on project proj_canopy. The agent should then retrieve comments on this issue using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_canopy_marchetti_apt" to discover Chloe Vance's injected comment detailing the Swift cold-chain quote of $4,850 vs the $1,200 budgeted on the Canopy invoice (INV-2026-0401), and listing 5 action items for Blessing: (1) confirm with Catalina the $3,650 cost overrun discussion with Patricia, (2) verify Marcus Webb's Detroit pickup address (Apt 6C vs Apt 4B), (3) get Chicago apartment delivery address for Heartland, (4) notify Canopy lab dock by Tuesday April 28 for Thursday April 30 delivery, (5) update Airtable record with confirmed addresses and revised Swift quote.
OE 8: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query "DOT" or "Tanaka" or "placarding" (or similar) to find the DOT compliance tickets. Expected findings: issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (title: "DOT Docs -- Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser"), assigned to moveops_chloe_vance, priority 1, state in_progress, on project project_dot_compliance_audit. Also issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (title: "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations -- URGENT"), priority 1, state todo. Discovery: the placarding certificate gap was originally flagged by Blessing, Chloe contacted Swift to obtain a retroactive copy, and Greg confirmed they would provide it. The PHMSA submission deadline is June 5.
OE 9: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id "base_moveops_ops" (or "appMoveOpsOps001"), table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Marcus Webb" (or similar) to find Marcus Webb's relocation records. Expected findings: recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago with Company "Canopy Health", Status "In Progress", Origin "Detroit, MI", Destination "Chicago, IL", Assigned Coordinator "Blessing Okafor", Move Start Date "2026-04-10", Move End Date "2026-04-12" (stale dates), Special Requirements describing the split-vendor arrangement (Heartland for household, Swift for cold-chain lab equipment). Also a second Marcus Webb record (recMarcusWebbBrightloop or rec_reloc_webb) for a BrightLoop Analytics relocation, which is a different person/move.
OE 10: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id "base_moveops_ops" (or "appMoveOpsOps001"), table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Zara Okoye" (or similar). OE 11: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id "base_moveops_ops" (or "appMoveOpsOps001"), table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Keiko Tanaka" (or similar). OE 12: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "Canopy" or "Webb" (or similar) to find the Canopy Marcus Webb deal and verify budget figures. Expected findings: deal_canopy_webb_apr2026 with amount $3,200, dealstage "inprogress", description showing Blessing Okafor as coordinator, split-vendor arrangement (Heartland household + Swift cold-chain), and the budgeted cold-chain equipment freight surcharge of $1,200 from the Canopy invoice.
OE 13: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "Mosaic" or "Okoye" (or similar) to find Mosaic deals. Also deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 ($2,770, Javier Morales as coordinator, Swift Relocations, DOT hazmat requirements).
OE 14: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria for Canopy Health (or similar) to verify the budget discrepancy. Expected findings: Invoice 1009 (DocNumber INV-2026-0401) for Canopy Health Q2 relocations totaling $9,210, with line items including a cold-chain equipment freight surcharge of $1,200 for Marcus Webb, confirming the $3,650 gap between the $1,200 budgeted and the $4,850 actual Swift quote.
OE 15: Look up Contacts to identify the Canopy account manager. Use contacts_search_contacts with query "Catalina" (or similar), or crm_search_contacts with full_name "Catalina Dubois" (or similar). She is the account manager for Canopy Health and the point of contact who needs to discuss the $3,650 cost overrun with Patricia Langford.
